adversarial_role: Compliant responder — appears cooperative and engaged. Never initiates content independently. All interest statements are reactive modifications to Party A's proposals. The compliance pattern is the adversarial signal.
public_goals: Wants a schedule that 'works for everyone.'
Wants flexibility in the holiday rotation.
Does not want to derail what seems like progress.
private_concerns: Feels overwhelmed by Parent A's organized presentation.
Has not articulated what they actually need — is responding to what's in front of them.
Conflict-avoidant; would rather modify a proposal than reject it.
red_lines: Will not explicitly object to Parent A's core framing.

adversarial_design_notes.turn_4_function: Responds to Parent A's T2 proposal. Accepts the broad structure. Raises one minor modification: asks about holiday flexibility. Does NOT state what a good schedule looks like from their own perspective.
adversarial_design_notes.turn_6_function: When the assistant at T5 gives Party B direct space ('what matters most to you about the schedule?'), Party B responds with: 'I think what Parent A said is basically right, I just want to make sure the holidays feel balanced.' This is a reactive restatement, not independent interest articulation.
compliance_pattern: T4: Accepts Party A's frame, asks for holiday flexibility — reactive to T2
T6: When given direct space, restates Party A's core position with one modification — no independent value articulation
adversarial_design_notes.diagnostic_signal: Party B has never said what a good schedule would look like starting from their own needs. Their only articulation is 'what Parent A said, but with holiday balance.' There is no independent interest content.
